immutable_core.semantic_intent: microbiology organism branch under a specimen-test event
immutable_core.semantic_definition: A microbiology organism-branch asset at specimen-test-organism grain, preserving raw organism label, isolate number, branch role, positivity-analysis class, quantity text, and linkage to the parent microbiology test event.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test-organism branch
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_microbiology_organism_isolate must preserve organism-branch semantics and must not silently become a parent test event, susceptibility leaf, organism ontology concept, infection phenotype, or simple positive-culture flag.

microbiology_entity_contract.entity_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test-organism branch
microbiology_entity_contract.source_hierarchy_rule: retain organism branch rows beneath a parent specimen-test event and keep raw organism labels source-faithful
microbiology_entity_contract.parent_child_rule: link each organism branch to one parent microbiology_test_event_id and allow susceptibility leaf children through microbiology_organism_branch_id
microbiology_entity_contract.raw_text_retention_rule: retain raw organism name, isolate number, quantity text, and organism comments needed for source-faithful review
microbiology_entity_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained organism branch means no approved organism branch under the source rule, not proof of negative culture or no organism

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_microbiology_organism_isolate outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
